title: Mechanical Door Repairers
description: Install, service, or repair automatic door mechanisms and hydraulic doors. Includes garage door mechanics.

tasks.text: Wind large springs with upward motion of arm.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Adjust doors to open or close with the correct amount of effort, or make simple adjustments to electric openers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Carry springs to tops of doors, using ladders or scaffolding, and attach springs to tracks to install spring systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Repair or replace worn or broken door parts, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Complete required paperwork, such as work orders, according to services performed or required.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Fasten angle iron back-hangers to ceilings and tracks, using fasteners or welding equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Collect payment upon job completion.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install door frames, rails, steel rolling curtains, electronic-eye mechanisms, or electric door openers and closers, using power tools, hand tools, and electronic test equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect job sites, assessing headroom, side room, or other conditions to determine appropriateness of door for a given location.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Assemble and fasten tracks to structures or bucks, using impact wrenches or welding equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set doors into place or stack hardware sections into openings after rail or track installation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate lifts, winches, or chain falls to move heavy curtain doors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove or disassemble defective automatic mechanical door closers, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fabricate replacements for worn or broken parts, using welders, lathes, drill presses, or shaping or milling machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare doors for hardware installation, such as drilling holes to install locks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Run low voltage wiring on ceiling surfaces, using insulated staples.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut door stops or angle irons to fit openings.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Study blueprints and schematic diagrams to determine appropriate methods of installing or repairing automated door openers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install dock seals, bumpers, or shelters.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Order replacement springs, sections, or slats.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Lubricate door closer oil chambers, and pack spindles with leather washers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set in and secure floor treadles for door-activating mechanisms, and connect power packs and electrical panelboards to treadles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cover treadles with carpeting or other floor covering materials, and test systems by operating treadles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Bore or cut holes in flooring as required for installation, using hand or power tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean door closer parts, using caustic soda, rotary brushes, or grinding wheels.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
